column1: # Nutrient RETENTION factors — what survives COOKING. Percent of the raw amount remaining.
# USDA Table of Nutrient Retention Factors Release 6 (2007) as shipped in the INDB repo
# (USDA_nrf.xlsx). Filtered to the food classes and the seven nutrients this system tracks.
# THIS IS NOT BIOAVAILABILITY. forms.csv answers 'how much gets absorbed' (phytate).
# This answers 'how much is still in the pot'. They multiply — they do not substitute:
#     delivered = raw x (retention/100) x (absorption from forms.csv)
# Read the two together or you will double-count a win. The single most useful row here is
# the boiled-and-DRAINED vs boiled-WATER-USED pair: draining dal water is where folate and
# potassium actually go. That is a cooking decision this table can settle and forms.csv cannot.
# ret_* = percent retained. Blank = USDA published no factor for that pair.
# class is USDA's own food grouping - it is coarse and Western. A dal is a legume here and
